methods.description: Redundancy Analysis (RDA) is used to analyze the variance in environmental data and classify stations based on pollution levels and environmental characteristics.

steps.description: The first two RDA axes account for 66.57 and 4.64% of the total variance. Since they explain a minor percentage of the total variations, higher axes were omitted.
steps.searchable_summary: perform RDA and select first two axes based on variance explained

steps.description: Axis 1 classifies stations into two groups depending on their environmental characteristics.
steps.searchable_summary: classify stations into groups based on Axis 1

steps.description: Group I occupy the positive area of axis 1. It consists of the most polluted stations of the Hurghada site.
steps.searchable_summary: identify Group I as most polluted stations

steps.description: Group II is located on the negative side of the triplot chart. It consists of the least polluted or non-polluted stations.
steps.searchable_summary: identify Group II as least polluted stations
